body.title: Strip ANSI escape sequences from Igor's console .log files
body.result: Root cause: tmux pipe-pane in TheIgors/igor captured raw terminal stream verbatim into console.log files. Fix: pipe through 'sed -u -E' with CSI + OSC strip regex. Live console keeps colors; .log files now plain text. 7 regression tests + drift-guard. Takes effect on next igor launch. TheIgors commit 956ab78a.
body.description:
Igor's console log files (the .log targets capturing what Rich prints to console.print) contain raw ANSI escape sequences (e.g. \x1b[31m for red). They render as garbage in plain text editors like Kate. Need to strip ANSI sequences when writing to .log files — terminal output stays colored, but the file capture is plain text.

**Affected files:** TBD — discovery step in sprint. Likely candidates: wherever Rich console is initialized (wild_igor/igor/main.py and wild_igor/igor/logging_setup.py), and any FileHandler/file-tee that captures console output. Search for 'force_terminal=True' and FileHandlers that pass-through Rich output.
**Design rules:** terminal output unchanged — colors stay in the live console; file output stripped — .log files readable in any editor. The simplest path is to use a regex strip (re.sub(r'\x1b\[[0-9;]*[a-zA-Z]', '', s)) in the file-writing seam. Rich also has Console(record=True).export_text() which strips natively if the seam allows it.
**Scope boundary:** in — strip ANSI from .log file writes only. Out — change the live console rendering, change log levels, restructure logging.
**Test plan:** test_log_file_has_no_ansi_escapes — write a colored Rich message, read the captured log file, assert no \x1b in contents. Plus a smoke test that the live console output still includes the escape (so we didn't accidentally strip the wrong stream).
